title: Level 979
wikitext:
{{Class 1|color-h=164|color-s=40%|color-l=67%|class=1|safety=Safe|security=Stable|entity=Minimal Entity Count|hexagon=1}}{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Level 979: "Kaleidoscope Studios"'''}}__NOTOC____NOEDITSECTION__{{Background|file=979plaza.jpeg}} '''Level 979''' is the 980th level of the Backrooms. == Description == '''Level 979''' takes on the appearance of an abandoned television studio, specifically one that seems to specialize in children's entertainment. Exact details of any production that may or may not have been created at the studio is unknown, but signage and other objects found scattered about suggest that all (conjectural) content was intended for a cable channel known simply as "Kaleidoscope". Broadcasts of the Kaleidoscope channel or any of its shows have not been discovered, and any attempts to record or document its existence have resulted in unexplained technical failures, leading to speculation that the channel operates outside conventional means of transmission. {{Blurimage|file=979plaza.jpeg|caption=A view of the studio from the outside.}} The level is always entered at the end of a long concrete corridor, in which a long, winding queue consisting of rope dividers leads to a turnstile at the end. This opens up into an outdoor plaza of sorts, with tall, unscalable cinder block walls preventing any attempt at escaping. It is always nighttime in this area. The only way forward is the main entrance to the studio, which leads to a spacious lobby. Although most interior design elements seem to have been stripped away during some unknown prior period, remnants of this assumed earlier version can still be seen throughout the studio. The architectural style of what remains can be best described as "wacky", with strange, sometimes surreal decor with no discernible purpose adorning the walls and ceiling. It somewhat resembles interiors from decades past designed to appeal to children, although the general sense of abandonment and decay makes this less apparent. A long hallway leads off from the lobby, lined with doors on either side. Each of these doors leads into a room where various sets apparently used in Kaleidoscope productions were located. Most props and other objects that might have identified what show was produced there have been removed by persons unknown, but enough clues remain to make educated guesses about the nature of these shows. Some of the rooms are large enough to contain multiple sets, while others house just one. A few rooms appear as if they have been converted into storage areas, containing crates, boxes, and other miscellaneous items. There is also a small break room located near the back of the hallway, with a non-functional coffee-maker and an empty vending machine. No other evidence of food, edible or otherwise, is found within the studio. At the far end of the hallway, a pair of double doors lead to what was presumably a control room for the studio. However, these doors are locked shut and no attempt to force them open has been successful thus far. There are windows along the top half of each door, allowing limited visibility into the room beyond. Only one studio light is functional however, casting a dim yellow glow onto the empty floor below. Some have claimed to have seen a solitary director's chair underneath the light, but these reports have not been confirmed. The rest of the room is in complete darkness, making further exploration difficult and potentially dangerous. All other rooms in the studio do not appear to serve any discernible function or purpose, and attempts to find any sort of map, blueprints, or other documentation relating to the studio's inner workings have proven fruitless thus far. The only clue that can be found is a small envelope stuffed behind a ventilation grate, containing several photocopied pages of handwritten notes. The handwriting is illegible, and attempts to decipher it have so far been unsuccessful. It is possible that these notes may hold the key to understanding more about '''Level 979''' and its mysterious history, if it has any. The floor plan beyond the lobby and sets is never consistent, as the layout changes on each visit. There are recurring rooms and objects however, such as one room that houses a blue screen, presumably used for special effects in Kaleidoscope productions. Explorers are advised to not approach this screen, as it leads to an endless blue void from which there is no escape. Another room houses various costumes and props, with racks of clothing and shelves overflowing with toys, masks, and other items. {{Blurimage|file=979shop.jpeg|caption=The gift shop.}} One room that appears unnervingly frequently is one that resembles a gift shop of sorts, though the shelves are usually devoid of any merchandise. One may occasionally find a small, cheaply-produced stuffed toy sitting on the counter, resembling a chimeric creature composed of various body parts stitched together, resulting in an animal that is both mammalian and reptilian in appearance. A price tag attached to the toy names the creature "Buzzo the Buzzo" and lists a price of $1.99 USD. These toys are, so far, the only items ever found in this room, and their presence is unsettling to say the least. The significance or purpose of Buzzo and the gift shop within the context of '''Level 979''', if any, remains unknown, but Buzzo itself seems to be the level's crude interpretation of what a cartoon character is supposed to look like. {{Blurimage|file=Buzzo.jpg|caption=Artist's sketch of Buzzo doll.}} Another recurring item elsewhere in the studio is a small 1980s CRT television that broadcasts only static. Upon closer inspection, the static has been found to be composed of faint, distorted images of various people and creatures. The images move and change constantly, making it difficult to discern any coherent narrative or pattern. The television emits a low humming noise, which some have reported to be unpleasantly loud and disorienting. Under no circumstances should one watch the static for more than a minute at a time, as it will quickly cause the viewer to stop and stare at the screen, unable to perform any other actions. As a result, those who are unprepared for this effect will remain in place indefinitely, eventually leading to starvation and death. Various signage and other objects suggest that a studio tour was once offered here, with interactive experiences and meet-and-greets with the on-air personalities from Kaleidoscope productions. Only empty display cases and torn posters now remain, along with large, brightly-colored buttons that can be pressed. These buttons do not seem to serve any obvious function, and there is no apparent feedback when they are pressed beyond a small LED light inside the button turning on and off. A single instance of a game show stage has been reported, with an obstacle course that needed to be traversed in order to reach the other side of the room. The wanderer claims that the floor was sticky with an unknown foul-smelling substance that had seeped into the cracks between the tiles. No other encounters with this room have been reported. Taking an elevator or the stairs will almost always lead to a floor containing upper management offices (when going up), or to a basement animation studio composed of rows upon rows of identical cubicles with animator's desks stationed at each of them (when going down). In either case, it is advised to not wander for too long, as there is usually only one way back to the ground floor and it is quite easy to get lost.
The general atmosphere of '''Level 979''' is one of eerie calm interrupted by occasional moments of unsettling strangeness. As noted above, wanderers who have spent extended periods of time within the studio often report feelings of disorientation and unease, coupled with vivid hallucinations involving the various characters and shows from Kaleidoscope productions. The longer one remains within '''Level 979''', the more these symptoms tend to intensify, eventually leading to full-blown psychosis in extreme cases. As a result, those who venture here should do so only with proper preparation and caution.
- [[M.E.G.|M.E.G.]] Research Report Team
- [[M.E.G.|M.E.G.]] Research Report Team
== Entities == Reports of entities within '''Level 979''', hostile or otherwise, are rare but not unknown. Usually these encounters take the form of fleeting glimpses of some of the more common Backrooms denizens such as [[Entity 3|Smilers]]. However, on extremely rare occasions, a massive creature resembling a 10-foot-tall Buzzo costume has been seen lurking in the shadows or peeking out from behind set pieces. This creature is believed to be an avatar of some sort for whatever force or being truly controls '''Level 979''', though what its intentions or desires are remain unclear. Attempts to engage this creature in conversation or other forms of interaction have always resulted in failure, with the creature simply vanishing into the shadows once it becomes aware of the presence of any living being. == Colonies and Outposts == '''Level 979''' has not been colonized as of this writing. While generally safe to travel through (provided one is well-prepared), reports of wanderers suffering ill effects during extended visits have deterred attempts to establish permanent settlements. Symptoms include disorientation, memory loss, auditory hallucinations, and an overwhelming sense of dread, all of which intensify the longer one remains in the level. These effects, combined with the unpredictable environment and lack of resources, make '''Level 979''' highly inhospitable for colonization efforts. That said, research has been ongoing to understand the underlying causes of these phenomena and to potentially mitigate their effects. In addition, these symptoms only manifest themselves after at least 3 days. == Entrances and Exits == {{Blurimage|file=979lot.jpeg|caption=The building viewed from the parking lot.}} === Entrances === * Off-ramps on [[Level 480]] sometimes lead to this level, usually marked with an archway with a sign reading "Kaleidoscope Studios". This entrance was how '''Level 979''' was first discovered. * Doors in [[Level 477]] marked with a Kaleidoscope logo will lead here. * Falling asleep on one of the chairs in the "prize room" area of [[Level 978|the previous level]] will send one here. === Exits === Exit signs can be found through out the studio, although most do not lead to any known exit point. When an exit sign is actually accompanied by a door, it usually leads to a dead end or another section of the studio. The only exception is the exit near the break room, which leads to a massive parking lot. Those who have escaped '''Level 979''' cannot recall how they were able to do so, and their accounts vary greatly regarding the details of their escape. This has led some investigators to believe that there may be more than one way out of the studio. Locating these exits remains a top priority for researchers studying '''Level 979'''. {{Redirect|Prev=978|Next=980}} {{Collapse|initial='''> Open Author & Licensing Information'''|final='''> Close Author & Licensing Information'''|body= '''Author''': [[User:Tymime|Tymime]]}} [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Safe]] [[Category:Cluster I]]
